mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.5% after a prolonged period near zero, the sudden removal of excess liquidity increases borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5%, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate demand-pull inflation that erodes real corporate margins, causing the S&P 500 to post negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL increase, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals an impending growth contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances during identified late-cycle months, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not below -0.2 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a two-month period, it indicates a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires, or the signal's mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, supporting positive earnings surprises and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns are not significantly associated with INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter annualized growth above 2% signals robust aggregate demand that translates into positive earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of triggered quarters, or the mean return conditional on GDPC1 acceleration is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
